title: Setuptools Register Command
content:
## `setuptools.command.register`

This module provides a custom `register` command for setuptools that overrides the default behavior to include a warning about the deprecation of `register` in favor of `twine` for uploading packages to PyPI.

### Purpose

The primary purpose of this command is to inform users that the `register` command is deprecated and guide them towards using `twine`, the recommended tool for package distribution.

### Key Exports/Functions

*   `register`: A class that inherits from `distutils.command.register.register` and overrides its `run` method.

    *   `run()`: This method first ensures that `egg_info` is run to get the latest package information. Then, it calls the original `register.run()` method. Finally, it prints a warning message to the console indicating that registering is deprecated and recommends using `twine`.

### Usage Example

When building and registering a package with setuptools, the `register` command will automatically display the deprecation warning:

```bash
python setup.py register
```

This will output something similar to:

```
running register
running egg_info
generating MYPACKAGE.egg-info/PKG-INFO
... (other output from original register command)
WARNING: Registering is deprecated, use twine to upload instead (https://pypi.org/p/twine/)
```

## `setuptools.command.register`

### Purpose

The primary purpose of this command is to inform users that the `register` command is deprecated and guide them towards using `twine`, the recommended tool for package distribution.

*   `register`: A class that inherits from `distutils.command.register.register` and overrides its `run` method.

    *   `run()`: This method first ensures that `egg_info` is run to get the latest package information. Then, it calls the original `register.run()` method. Finally, it prints a warning message to the console indicating that registering is deprecated and recommends using `twine`.

### Usage Example

When building and registering a package with setuptools, the `register` command will automatically display the deprecation warning:
